macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin falls sharply amid ETF outflows and broader crypto selloff
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped toward the mid-$60,000 range as spot Bitcoin ETFs logged prolonged outflows and the market faced a broader liquidation wave. Related reports also linked the move to Mt. Gox-related transfers and heightened geopolitical risk affecting risk assets.
items.source: NewsNow / WION News / Coinotag / Finance in Bold

items.title: Treasury sanctions major Iranian cryptocurrency exchange
items.summary: U.S. authorities expanded sanctions pressure on Iran’s crypto sector, with reports describing a major Iranian cryptocurrency exchange as a target. The move adds fresh regulatory and geopolitical pressure to the digital-asset market.

items.title: SEC digital-assets strategy signals continued focus on crypto oversight
items.summary: The SEC’s 2026–2030 strategic plan places digital assets at the center of its regulatory agenda, underscoring that crypto oversight remains a priority. This adds to the day’s policy focus alongside other U.S. regulatory developments.

items.title: Mastercard expands stablecoin settlement support
items.summary: Mastercard reportedly expanded stablecoin settlement support to include Ripple’s RLUSD and Circle’s USDC, highlighting continued integration of crypto rails into mainstream payments. Separate reporting also said Mastercard is working with Visa and Stripe on stablecoin-related collaboration.

items.title: Crypto industry lobbying intensifies around U.S. legislation
items.summary: Defend Developers launched a PAC to support crypto developers as the CLARITY Act advances, while Coin Center said the BRCA is ready to become law. These developments point to active lobbying and legislative positioning across the U.S. crypto policy landscape.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta reportedly hires four more OpenAI researchers
items.summary: Meta has reportedly recruited Shengjia Zhao, Jiahui Yu, Shuchao Bi, and Hongyu Ren from OpenAI, adding to its aggressive AI talent push. The move underscores the intensifying competition among frontier-model labs for specialized research staff.

items.title: Apple to open up underlying AI technology to developers
items.summary: Apple is reportedly preparing to expose more of its underlying AI technology to third-party developers, a step that could expand the ecosystem around its on-device and cloud AI features. The announcement points to a broader trend of major platforms turning foundation-model capabilities into developer platforms.

items.title: OpenAI’s ChatGPT experiences outages
items.summary: Users reported problems loading prompts in ChatGPT, indicating a service disruption affecting one of the most widely used AI products. The issue highlights the operational fragility of large-scale AI services as usage grows.

items.title: US-UAE AI data campus deal remains unresolved
items.summary: Sources say the multi-billion-dollar AI data campus deal between the US and UAE is still far from finalized. The report reflects ongoing geopolitical and infrastructure competition around advanced AI compute capacity.

items.title: Generalist raises $400M for AI robotics expansion
items.summary: Generalist, a US AI robotics company, raised $400 million at a reported $2 billion valuation to accelerate work on physical general intelligence. The round included NVentures, Bezos Expeditions, Fei-Fei Li, and Zoom CEO Eric Yuan, and the company said the capital will support new models, data infrastructure, and commercialization.

items.title: Recent arXiv machine learning papers point to active research on CoT, reinforcement learning, and diffusion models
items.summary: Recent arXiv submissions include work on chain-of-thought theory in in-context learning, stabilizing linear Q-learning with target updates, and training diffusion models on incomplete tabular data. These preprints suggest continued momentum in core ML theory, reinforcement learning, and generative modeling.

items.title: Scientists question the expanding role of AI in astrophysics research
items.summary: A Science article highlights growing debate over how far AI tools should be integrated into astrophysics workflows, with some researchers warning about the risk of reducing human scientific contribution. The piece reflects a wider industry and academic trend: AI adoption is accelerating faster than consensus on governance and research norms.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
